note: Teglarsiz. Ketma-ket rejim, har run 3 post (variety uchun turlar aralash). Faqat tekshirilgan ilmiy/tarixiy faktlar.

posts.title: 3 Powerful Verbs
posts.text:
рЯФ§ WORD OF THE DAY

рЯУМ achieve /…ЩЋИt ГiЋРv/ вАФ erishmoq
рЯУМ improve /…™mЋИpruЋРv/ вАФ yaxshilamoq
рЯУМ overcome /ЋМ…Щ Кv…ЩЋИk Мm/ вАФ yengmoq, bartaraf etmoq

рЯТђ With daily practice, you can overcome any difficulty and achieve your goals.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Har kunlik mashq bilan har qanday qiyinchilikni yengib, maqsadingizga erishasiz.

posts.title: Octopus has 3 hearts
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

An octopus has three hearts and blue blood. Two hearts pump blood to the gills, and one pumps it to the rest of the body.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Sakkizoyoqning uchta yuragi va ko'k qoni bor. Ikki yurak qonni jabralarga, biri esa butun tanaga haydaydi.

рЯУЪ Murakkab so'zlar: gills /…°…™lz/ вАФ jabralar; pump /p Мmp/ вАФ haydamoq

posts.title: Cleopatra & the Pyramids
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Cleopatra lived closer in time to the first Moon landing (1969) than to the building of the Great Pyramid. The Pyramid was completed around 2560 BC вАФ about 2,500 years before her birth.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Kleopatra Buyuk Piramida qurilishidan (mil. av. ~2560) ko'ra, 1969-yilgi Oyga qo'nishga vaqt jihatdan yaqinroq yashagan. Piramida undan ~2500 yil oldin qurilgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: ancient /ЋИe…™n Г…Щnt/ вАФ qadimiy; century /ЋИsent Г…Щri/ вАФ asr

posts.title: The Lion and the Mouse
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Lion and the Mouse

A tiny mouse woke a sleeping lion. The lion caught it, but the mouse begged for mercy, so the lion let it go. Later, hunters trapped the lion in a net. The little mouse heard him roar and chewed the ropes until the lion was free.

рЯТ° Moral: Even the small can help the mighty.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Kichik sichqon uxlayotgan sherni uyg'otadi. Sher uni kechiradi. Keyin sher to'rga tushganda, sichqon arqonlarni kemirib uni qutqaradi. Xulosa: kichik ham kuchliga yordam bera oladi.

рЯУЪ mercy /ЋИm…ЬЋРsi/ вАФ rahm; net /net/ вАФ to'r; chew /t ГuЋР/ вАФ kemirmoq

posts.title: Grammar Quiz
posts.text:
рЯУЭ GRAMMAR TEST

She ____ to school every day.

A) go
B) goes
C) going
D) gone

рЯТ≠ Javobni o'ylab toping! рЯСЗ
posts.answer:
вЬЕ To'g'ri javob: B) goes

рЯУЦ Present Simple'da he/she/it bilan fe'lga -s/-es qo'shiladi: she goes, he reads, it works.

posts.title: Samarkand вАФ 2750 years
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Samarkand is over 2,750 years old вАФ one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in Central Asia. It was a key city on the Silk Road, and in 1370 the ruler Amir Temur (Tamerlane) made it his capital.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Samarqand 2750 yildan oshiq tarixga ega вАФ Markaziy Osiyodagi eng qadimiy shaharlardan biri. Buyuk Ipak yo'lining muhim shahri; 1370-yilda Amir Temur uni poytaxt qilgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: inhabited /…™nЋИh√¶b…™t…™d/ вАФ aholi yashaydigan; capital /ЋИk√¶p…™tl/ вАФ poytaxt

posts.title: Lightning vs the Sun
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

A bolt of lightning can reach about 30,000¬∞C вАФ that is roughly five times hotter than the surface of the Sun (~5,500¬∞C).

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Chaqmoq harorati ~30,000¬∞C ga yetadi вАФ bu Quyosh sirtidan (~5,500¬∞C) qariyb besh barobar issiqroq.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: lightning /ЋИla…™tn…™≈Л/ вАФ chaqmoq; surface /ЋИs…ЬЋРf…™s/ вАФ sirt, yuza

posts.title: IELTS Trend Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ IELTS VOCABULARY

рЯУМ significant /s…™…°ЋИn…™f…™k…Щnt/ вАФ sezilarli, muhim
рЯУМ gradual /ЋИ…°r√¶d Тu…Щl/ вАФ bosqichma-bosqich
рЯУМ fluctuate /ЋИfl Мkt Гue…™t/ вАФ o'zgarib turmoq

рЯТђ Prices fluctuated in spring but showed a significant rise by the end of the year.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Bahorda narxlar o'zgarib turdi, lekin yil oxiriga sezilarli o'sdi.

posts.title: The Tortoise and the Hare
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Tortoise and the Hare

A fast hare laughed at a slow tortoise and challenged him to a race. The hare ran ahead, then stopped to nap, sure he would win. The tortoise kept moving slowly without stopping вАФ and crossed the finish line first.

рЯТ° Moral: Slow and steady wins the race.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Tez quyon sekin toshbaqani masxara qilib, poygaga chaqiradi. Quyon uxlab qoladi; toshbaqa to'xtamay yurib, birinchi keladi. Xulosa: sekin, lekin barqaror harakat g'alaba keltiradi.

рЯУЪ hare /he…Щ/ вАФ quyon; nap /n√¶p/ вАФ mizg'imoq; steady /ЋИstedi/ вАФ barqaror

posts.title: Bananas are berries
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Botanically, a banana is a berry, but a strawberry is not! A true berry grows from one flower with one ovary вАФ bananas qualify, strawberries do not.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Botanika nuqtai nazaridan banan вАФ rezavor meva (berry), lekin qulupnay emas! Haqiqiy berry bitta gulning bitta tugunchasidan o'sadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: berry /ЋИberi/ вАФ rezavor meva; flower /ЋИfla К…Щ/ вАФ gul

posts.title: Idiom: Piece of cake
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯН∞ "a piece of cake" вАФ juda oson ish

рЯТђ The test was a piece of cake for her.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ So'zma-so'z "bir bo'lak tort" emas вАФ ma'nosi "juda oson". Suhbatda tabiiy eshitiladi: The exam was a piece of cake.

рЯУЪ piece /piЋРs/ вАФ bo'lak; easy /ЋИiЋРzi/ вАФ oson

posts.title: Shortest war: 38 min
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

The shortest war in recorded history was the Anglo-Zanzibar War (27 August 1896). It lasted only about 38 minutes, from 09:02 to 09:40.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Tarixdagi eng qisqa urush вАФ Angliya-Zanzibar urushi (1896-yil 27-avgust). U atigi ~38 daqiqa davom etgan (soat 09:02 dan 09:40 gacha).

рЯУЪ So'zlar: war /w…ФЋР/ вАФ urush; recorded /r…™ЋИk…ФЋРd…™d/ вАФ qayd etilgan; last /l…СЋРst/ вАФ davom etmoq

posts.title: Advanced Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ ADVANCED VOCABULARY

рЯУМ inevitable /…™nЋИev…™t…Щbl/ вАФ muqarrar
рЯУМ profound /pr…ЩЋИfa Кnd/ вАФ chuqur, kuchli
рЯУМ mitigate /ЋИm…™t…™…°e…™t/ вАФ yumshatmoq, kamaytirmoq

рЯТђ Technology has a profound impact on society, and change is inevitable.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Texnologiya jamiyatga chuqur ta'sir qiladi, o'zgarish esa muqarrar.

posts.title: Sunlight takes 8 minutes
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth. So you always see the Sun as it looked 8 minutes ago!

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Quyosh nuri Yerga yetib kelishi uchun ~8 daqiqa 20 soniya ketadi. Demak siz Quyoshni doim 8 daqiqa oldingi holatida ko'rasiz!

рЯУЪ So'zlar: light /la…™t/ вАФ nur, yorug'lik; reach /riЋРt Г/ вАФ yetib bormoq

posts.title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Boy Who Cried Wolf

A shepherd boy often shouted "Wolf!" as a joke, and villagers ran to help вАФ but there was no wolf. One day a real wolf came. The boy shouted, but this time nobody believed him.

рЯТ° Moral: Liars are not believed, even when they tell the truth.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Cho'pon bola hazillashib "Bo'ri!" deb baqirardi. Bir kuni haqiqiy bo'ri kelganda, hech kim ishonmadi. Xulosa: yolg'onchiga rost aytganda ham ishonishmaydi.

рЯУЪ shepherd /ЋИ Гep…Щd/ вАФ cho'pon; villager /ЋИv…™l…™d Т…Щ/ вАФ qishloq aholisi; believe /b…™ЋИliЋРv/ вАФ ishonmoq

posts.title: Conditional Quiz
posts.text:
рЯУЭ GRAMMAR TEST

If I ____ more time, I would travel the world.

A) have
B) had
C) will have
D) having

рЯТ≠ Javob рЯСЗ
posts.answer:
вЬЕ To'g'ri javob: B) had

рЯУЦ Second Conditional (hozirgi noreal holat): If + Past Simple, ... would + V1. "If I had time, I would travel."

posts.title: Oxford older than Aztecs
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Teaching at the University of Oxford began around 1096. The Aztec capital, Tenochtitl√°n, was founded in 1325 вАФ so Oxford was already over two centuries old before the Aztec city existed.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Oksford universitetida dars ~1096-yilda boshlangan. Atstek poytaxti Tenochtitlan esa 1325-yilda asos solingan вАФ ya'ni Oksford undan 200 yildan oshiq oldin mavjud edi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: founded /ЋИfa Кnd…™d/ вАФ asos solingan; century /ЋИsent Г…Щri/ вАФ asr

posts.title: Sharks older than trees
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Sharks are older than trees. Sharks have existed for about 450 million years, while the first true trees appeared around 385 million years ago.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Sharklar (akula) daraxtlardan ham qadimiy. Sharklar ~450 million yildan beri mavjud, birinchi haqiqiy daraxtlar esa ~385 million yil avval paydo bo'lgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: appear /…ЩЋИp…™…Щ/ вАФ paydo bo'lmoq; million /ЋИm…™lj…Щn/ вАФ million

posts.title: Phrasal Verbs
posts.text:
рЯФ§ PHRASAL VERBS

рЯУМ give up вАФ voz kechmoq
рЯУМ find out вАФ bilib olmoq
рЯУМ carry on вАФ davom ettirmoq

рЯТђ Never give up вАФ find out your weak points and carry on practising.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Hech qachon voz kechmang вАФ zaif tomonlaringizni aniqlang va mashqni davom ettiring.

posts.title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Ant and the Grasshopper

All summer the grasshopper sang while the ant stored food for winter. When the cold came, the grasshopper was hungry and the ant was safe and warm.

рЯТ° Moral: Prepare today for the needs of tomorrow.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yoz bo'yi chigirtka qo'shiq aytdi, chumoli esa qishga oziq yig'di. Qish kelganda chumoli to'q, chigirtka och qoldi. Xulosa: ertangi kun uchun bugundan tayyorlan.

рЯУЪ store /st…ФЋР/ вАФ saqlamoq, jamg'armoq; hungry /ЋИh М≈Л…°ri/ вАФ och; prepare /pr…™ЋИpe…Щ/ вАФ tayyorlanmoq

posts.title: Water expands when frozen
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Most liquids shrink when they get cold, but water expands by about 9% when it freezes. That is why ice floats on water.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ko'p suyuqliklar sovuganda qisqaradi, lekin suv muzlaganda ~9% ga kengayadi. Shu sababli muz suv ustida suzadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: expand /…™kЋИsp√¶nd/ вАФ kengaymoq; freeze /friЋРz/ вАФ muzlamoq; float /fl…Щ Кt/ вАФ suzmoq

posts.title: Great Wall myth
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

It is a popular myth that the Great Wall of China can be seen from space with the naked eye. In reality, it is too narrow to see without help, even from low orbit.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Buyuk Xitoy devorini koinotdan oddiy ko'z bilan ko'rish mumkin degan fikr вАФ afsona. Aslida u juda ingichka, hatto past orbitadan ham yordamchi asbobsiz ko'rinmaydi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: myth /m…™ќЄ/ вАФ afsona; narrow /ЋИn√¶r…Щ К/ вАФ ingichka, tor

posts.title: Describe Emotions
posts.text:
рЯФ§ VOCABULARY ¬Ј Feelings

рЯУМ anxious /ЋИ√¶≈Лk Г…Щs/ вАФ xavotirli
рЯУМ grateful /ЋИ…°re…™tfl/ вАФ minnatdor
рЯУМ confident /ЋИk…Тnf…™d…Щnt/ вАФ ishonchli, dadil

рЯТђ I felt anxious before the exam, but now I am confident and grateful for my practice.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Imtihondan oldin xavotirda edim, endi esa ishonchliman va mashqim uchun minnatdorman.

posts.title: A day longer than a year
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

On Venus, a day is longer than a year. Venus rotates so slowly that one full rotation (about 243 Earth days) takes longer than one orbit around the Sun (about 225 Earth days).

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Veneradagi bir kun bir yildan uzun. Venera shunchalik sekin aylanadiki, bir marta o'q atrofida aylanishi (~243 Yer kuni) Quyosh atrofini aylanib chiqishidan (~225 Yer kuni) uzoqroq.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: rotate /r…Щ КЋИte…™t/ вАФ aylanmoq; orbit /ЋИ…ФЋРb…™t/ вАФ orbita

posts.title: The Fox and the Grapes
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Fox and the Grapes

A hungry fox saw juicy grapes hanging high on a vine. He jumped again and again but could not reach them. At last he walked away, saying, "They are probably sour anyway."

рЯТ° Moral: It is easy to despise what you cannot have.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Och tulki baland uzumga sakraydi, lekin yetolmaydi. Ketayotib: "Baribir nordon bo'lsa kerak", deydi. Xulosa: yeta olmagan narsani kamsitish oson.

рЯУЪ vine /va…™n/ вАФ tok; reach /riЋРt Г/ вАФ yetmoq; sour /ЋИsa К…Щ/ вАФ nordon

posts.title: Present Perfect
posts.text:
рЯУЪ GRAMMAR ¬Ј Present Perfect

have/has + V3 вЖТ o'tmishdagi ish, hozir bilan bog'liq natija.

рЯТђ I have finished my homework. She has lived here for five years.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ "for" muddatni bildiradi (for 5 years), "since" boshlanish nuqtasini (since 2020).

рЯУЪ finish /ЋИf…™n…™ Г/ вАФ tugatmoq; result /r…™ЋИz Мlt/ вАФ natija

posts.title: Honey never spoils
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old вАФ and still edible!

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Asal hech qachon buzilmaydi. Arxeologlar qadimgi Misr maqbaralaridan 3000 yildan oshiq asal topishgan вАФ u hali ham yeyishga yaroqli!

рЯУЪ So'zlar: spoil /sp…Ф…™l/ вАФ buzilmoq; tomb /tuЋРm/ вАФ maqbara; edible /ЋИed…Щbl/ вАФ yeyish mumkin bo'lgan

posts.title: The first programmer
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

The world's first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, in the 1840s. She wrote an algorithm for Charles Babbage's mechanical machine, a century before modern computers existed.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Dunyodagi birinchi dasturchi вАФ Ada Lovelace (1840-yillar). U Charlz Bebbijning mexanik mashinasi uchun algoritm yozgan вАФ zamonaviy kompyuterlardan bir asr oldin.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: algorithm /ЋИ√¶l…°…Щr…™√∞…Щm/ вАФ algoritm; machine /m…ЩЋИ ГiЋРn/ вАФ mashina

posts.title: Essay Connectors
posts.text:
рЯФ§ IELTS WRITING CONNECTORS

рЯУМ furthermore /ЋИf…ЬЋР√∞…Щm…ФЋР/ вАФ bundan tashqari
рЯУМ nevertheless /ЋМnev…Щ√∞…ЩЋИles/ вАФ shunga qaramay
рЯУМ consequently /ЋИk…Тns…™kw…Щntli/ вАФ natijada

рЯТђ The plan was costly; nevertheless, it succeeded. Consequently, profits rose.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Reja qimmat edi; shunga qaramay, muvaffaqiyatli bo'ldi. Natijada foyda oshdi.

posts.title: The Golden Touch
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Golden Touch

King Midas wished that everything he touched would turn to gold. At first he was happy, but then his food and even his daughter turned to gold. He begged to lose the gift.

рЯТ° Moral: Greed can destroy what we love most.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Shoh Midas te–Ї–Їan narsasi oltinga aylanishini so'raydi. Lekin ovqati, hatto qizi ham oltinga aylanadi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik eng qadrli narsani yo'q qilishi mumkin.

рЯУЪ wish /w…™ Г/ вАФ orzu qilmoq; greed /…°riЋРd/ вАФ ochko'zlik; destroy /d…™ЋИstr…Ф…™/ вАФ yo'q qilmoq

posts.title: The Eiffel Tower grows
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer. Heat makes the iron expand, so the metal structure rises slightly when it is hot.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Eyfel minorasi yozda ~15 sm balandroq bo'lishi mumkin. Issiqlik temirni kengaytiradi, shuning uchun metall tuzilma biroz ko'tariladi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: iron /ЋИa…™…Щn/ вАФ temir; expand /…™kЋИsp√¶nd/ вАФ kengaymoq; structure /ЋИstr Мkt Г…Щ/ вАФ tuzilma

posts.title: Synonym Quiz
posts.text:
рЯУЭ IELTS READING TEST

"The results were remarkable."
remarkable = ?

A) ordinary
B) impressive
C) boring
D) confusing

рЯТ≠ Javob рЯСЗ
posts.answer:
вЬЕ To'g'ri javob: B) impressive

рЯУЦ remarkable = ajoyib, e'tiborga loyiq (impressive). Reading'da sinonimlarni bilish juda muhim.

posts.title: Bukhara вАФ Silk Road jewel
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Bukhara has more than 2,000 years of history and was a major centre of learning on the Silk Road. Its old city is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with hundreds of preserved monuments.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Buxoro 2000 yildan ortiq tarixga ega va Ipak yo'lidagi yirik ilm markazi bo'lgan. Eski shahri YuNESKO Jahon merosi ro'yxatida, yuzlab yodgorliklari saqlangan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: heritage /ЋИher…™t…™d Т/ вАФ meros; preserve /pr…™ЋИz…ЬЋРv/ вАФ saqlamoq

posts.title: Time Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ VOCABULARY ¬Ј Time

рЯУМ rarely /ЋИre…Щli/ вАФ kamdan-kam
рЯУМ frequently /ЋИfriЋРkw…Щntli/ вАФ tez-tez
рЯУМ eventually /…™ЋИvent Гu…Щli/ вАФ oxir-oqibat

рЯТђ I rarely watched films in English before, but I do it frequently now, and eventually I understood them easily.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Avval kamdan-kam ingliz tilida film ko'rardim, endi tez-tez ko'raman va oxir-oqibat ularni oson tushunadigan bo'ldim.

posts.title: The waggle dance
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Honeybees "talk" by dancing. A bee performs a special "waggle dance" to tell other bees the direction and distance to flowers. This discovery won a Nobel Prize.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Asalarilar raqs orqali "gaplashadi". Ari maxsus "waggle" raqsi bilan gullarning yo'nalishi va masofasini boshqalarga aytadi. Bu kashfiyot Nobel mukofotini olgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: direction /d…ЩЋИrek Гn/ вАФ yo'nalish; distance /ЋИd…™st…Щns/ вАФ masofa; discovery /d…™ЋИsk Мv…Щri/ вАФ kashfiyot

posts.title: The Two Travellers
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Two Travellers and the Bear

Two friends were walking when a bear appeared. One climbed a tree; the other lay still and pretended to be dead. The bear sniffed him and left. "What did it whisper?" asked the first. "It said: never trust a friend who leaves you in danger."

рЯТ° Moral: True friends stay with you in trouble.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ayiq kel–≥–∞–љ–і–∞ biri daraxtga chiqdi, ikkinchisi o'likka soxta o'xshatdi. Xulosa: haqiqiy do'st qiyinchilikda tashlab ketmaydi.

рЯУЪ pretend /pr…™ЋИtend/ вАФ o'zini ... qilib ko'rsatmoq; whisper /ЋИw…™sp…Щ/ вАФ pichirlamoq; trust /tr Мst/ вАФ ishonmoq

posts.title: Passive Voice
posts.text:
рЯУЪ GRAMMAR ¬Ј Passive Voice

be + V3 вЖТ ish kimga/nimaga qilingani muhim bo'lganda.

рЯТђ English is spoken in many countries. The bridge was built in 1990.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ IELTS Writing'da rasmiy uslub uchun foydali: "The data was collected from 500 people."

рЯУЪ spoken /ЋИsp…Щ Кk…Щn/ вАФ gapiriladigan; built /b…™lt/ вАФ qurilgan

posts.title: Your brain & languages
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Learning a second language strengthens the brain. Research shows that bilingual people often have better memory and focus, and may delay age-related memory decline.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ikkinchi tilni o'rganish miyani kuchaytiradi. Tadqiqotlarga ko'ra, ikki tilli odamlarda xotira va diqqat ko'pincha kuchliroq, yoshga bog'liq xotira pasayishi kechikishi mumkin.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: bilingual /ba…™ЋИl…™≈Л…°w…Щl/ вАФ ikki tilli; memory /ЋИmem…Щri/ вАФ xotira; decline /d…™ЋИkla…™n/ вАФ pasayish

posts.title: Idiom: Hit the books
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯУЪ "hit the books" вАФ qattiq o'qishga kirishmoq

рЯТђ I have an exam tomorrow, so I need to hit the books tonight.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ma'nosi "kitobni urmoq" emas вАФ "jiddiy o'qishga kirishmoq". Imtihon oldidan ishlatiladi.

рЯУЪ exam /…™…°ЋИz√¶m/ вАФ imtihon; tonight /t…ЩЋИna…™t/ вАФ bugun kechqurun

posts.title: Olympic Games origin
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

The Olympic Games began in ancient Greece in 776 BC, in the town of Olympia. They were held every four years in honour of the god Zeus вАФ a tradition that inspired the modern Olympics.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Olimpiada o'yinlari qadimgi Yunonistonda, mil. av. 776-yilda Olimpiya shahrida boshlangan. Har to'rt yilda Zevs xudosi sharafiga o'tkazilgan вАФ bu an'ana zamonaviy Olimpiadaga ilhom bergan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: honour /ЋИ…Тn…Щ/ вАФ sharaf; tradition /tr…ЩЋИd…™ Гn/ вАФ an'ana; inspire /…™nЋИspa…™…Щ/ вАФ ilhomlantirmoq

posts.title: Al-Khwarizmi & algebra
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Al-Khwarizmi (around 780-850), a scholar from Khwarazm, is called the father of algebra. The word 'algebra' comes from his book title, and the word 'algorithm' comes from his name.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Xorazmlik olim Al-Xorazmiy (~780-850) algebra otasi hisoblanadi. 'Algebra' so'zi uning kitobi nomidan, 'algoritm' so'zi esa uning ismidan kelib chiqqan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: scholar /ЋИsk…Тl…Щ/ вАФ olim; equation /…™ЋИkwe…™ Тn/ вАФ tenglama

posts.title: Babies have more bones
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Babies are born with about 300 bones, but adults have only 206. As we grow, some bones fuse together.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Chaqaloqlar ~300 ta suyak bilan tug'iladi, lekin kattalarda atigi 206 ta bo'ladi. O'sish davomida ba'zi suyaklar birlashib ketadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: bone /b…Щ Кn/ вАФ suyak; fuse /fjuЋРz/ вАФ birlashmoq; grow /…°r…Щ К/ вАФ o'smoq

posts.title: The Crow and the Pitcher
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Crow and the Pitcher

A thirsty crow found a pitcher with a little water at the bottom. Its beak could not reach it. So the clever crow dropped small stones inside, one by one, until the water rose to the top and it could drink.

рЯТ° Moral: Little by little, cleverness solves big problems.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Chanqagan qarg'a tubida ozgina suvi bor ko'zani topadi. Aql bilan toshlarni birma-bir tashlab, suvni ko'taradi va ichadi. Xulosa: aql-zakovat katta muammoni ham yechadi.

рЯУЪ thirsty /ЋИќЄ…ЬЋРsti/ вАФ chanqagan; beak /biЋРk/ вАФ tumshuq; clever /ЋИklev…Щ/ вАФ aqlli

posts.title: Antarctica is a desert
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

The largest desert on Earth is not hot вАФ it is Antarctica. A desert is defined by very low rainfall, not by heat, and Antarctica is extremely dry.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yerdagi eng katta cho'l issiq emas вАФ bu Antarktida. Cho'l issiqlik bilan emas, juda kam yog'in bilan belgilanadi, Antarktida esa o'ta quruq.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: desert /ЋИdez…Щt/ вАФ cho'l; rainfall /ЋИre…™nf…ФЋРl/ вАФ yog'in; dry /dra…™/ вАФ quruq

posts.title: Describe Change
posts.text:
рЯФ§ IELTS VOCABULARY ¬Ј Change

рЯУМ rise /ra…™z/ вАФ ko'tarilmoq
рЯУМ drop /dr…Тp/ вАФ tushmoq
рЯУМ remain stable вАФ barqaror qolmoq

рЯТђ Sales rose sharply, then dropped, and finally remained stable.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Sotuvlar keskin ko'tarildi, keyin tushdi va nihoyat barqaror qoldi.

posts.title: Ibn Sina (Avicenna)
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Ibn Sina (Avicenna, 980-1037), born near Bukhara, was one of history's greatest thinkers. His book 'The Canon of Medicine' was used in European universities for over 600 years.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Buxoro yaqinida tug'ilgan Ibn Sino (980-1037) tarixning buyuk allomalaridan biri. Uning 'Tib qonunlari' asari Yevropa universitetlarida 600 yildan ortiq o'qitilgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: medicine /ЋИmedsn/ вАФ tibbiyot; thinker /ЋИќЄ…™≈Лk…Щ/ вАФ mutafakkir

posts.title: Idiom: Break a leg
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯНА 'break a leg' вАФ omad tilayman (sahna/imtihon oldidan)

рЯТђ You have a speaking test today? Break a leg!
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ So'zma-so'z 'oyog'ingni sindir' emas вАФ ma'nosi 'omad tilayman'. Aktyorlar va talabalarga aytiladi.

рЯУЪ luck /l Мk/ вАФ omad; stage /ste…™d Т/ вАФ sahna

posts.title: No sound in space
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

There is no sound in space. Sound needs a medium like air or water to travel, but space is a vacuum вАФ almost empty.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Koinotda ovoz yo'q. Ovoz tarqalishi uchun havo yoki suv kabi muhit kerak, koinot esa vakuum вАФ deyarli bo'sh.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: sound /sa Кnd/ вАФ ovoz; vacuum /ЋИv√¶kjuЋРm/ вАФ vakuum; empty /ЋИempti/ вАФ bo'sh

posts.title: The North Wind and the Sun
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The North Wind and the Sun

The wind and the sun argued who was stronger. They tried to make a traveller remove his coat. The wind blew hard, but the man held his coat tighter. Then the sun shone warmly, and the man took it off himself.

рЯТ° Moral: Kindness is stronger than force.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Shamol va quyosh kim kuchli deb bahslashadi. Shamol qancha ursa, yo'lovchi paltosini mahkam ushlaydi; quyosh iliq nur sochganda, u o'zi yechadi. Xulosa: mehribonlik kuchdan kuchli.

рЯУЪ argue /ЋИ…СЋР…°juЋР/ вАФ bahslashmoq; blow /bl…Щ К/ вАФ esmoq; force /f…ФЋРs/ вАФ kuch, zo'rlik

posts.title: Saturn could float
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Saturn is so light for its size that it would float in water. Its average density is lower than water вАФ it is mostly gas.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Saturn hajmiga nisbatan shunchalik yengilki, u suvda suzib qolardi. Uning o'rtacha zichligi suvdan past вАФ u asosan gazdan iborat.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: float /fl…Щ Кt/ вАФ suzmoq; density /ЋИdens…Щti/ вАФ zichlik; gas /…°√¶s/ вАФ gaz

posts.title: Ulugh Beg the astronomer
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Ulugh Beg (1394-1449), ruler of Samarkand, was also a great astronomer. He built a huge observatory and measured the stars so accurately that his star catalogue was used for centuries.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Samarqand hukmdori Ulug'bek (1394-1449) buyuk astronom ham edi. U ulkan rasadxona qurib, yulduzlarni shunchalik aniq o'lchaganki, uning yulduzlar jadvalidan asrlar davomida foydalanilgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: astronomer /…ЩЋИstr…Тn…Щm…Щ/ вАФ astronom; observatory /…ЩbЋИz…ЬЋРv…Щtri/ вАФ rasadxona; accurate /ЋИ√¶kj…Щr…Щt/ вАФ aniq

posts.title: Opinion Phrases
posts.text:
рЯФ§ SPEAKING PHRASES ¬Ј Opinion

рЯУМ In my opinion вАФ mening fikrimcha
рЯУМ I believe that вАФ menimcha
рЯУМ It seems to me вАФ menga shunday tuyuladi

рЯТђ In my opinion, reading every day is the best way to learn.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ IELTS Speaking'da fikr bildirishda bu iboralar tabiiy eshitiladi.

posts.title: The fastest land animal
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

The cheetah is the fastest land animal. It can run up to about 110 km/h in short bursts to catch its prey.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Gepard quruqlikdagi eng tez hayvon. U o'ljasini tutish uchun qisqa masofada soatiga ~110 km tezlikda yugura oladi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: fast /f…СЋРst/ вАФ tez; prey /pre…™/ вАФ o'lja; catch /k√¶t Г/ вАФ tutmoq

posts.title: a / an / the
posts.text:
рЯУЪ GRAMMAR ¬Ј Articles

'a/an' вАФ noaniq (birinchi marta): a book, an apple.
'the' вАФ aniq (ma'lum narsa): the book on my desk.

рЯТђ I saw a dog. The dog was friendly.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ 'a' undosh tovushdan oldin, 'an' unli tovushdan oldin: a car, an hour.

рЯУЪ noun /na Кn/ вАФ ot; vowel /ЋИva К…Щl/ вАФ unli

posts.title: The Goose and Golden Eggs
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Goose with Golden Eggs

A farmer had a goose that laid one golden egg every day. Greedy for more, he cut the goose open to get all the gold at once вАФ but found nothing, and lost the goose.

рЯТ° Moral: Greed often destroys the source of good fortune.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Dehqonning g'ozi har kuni bitta oltin tuxum qo'yardi. Ochko'zlik qilib, hammasini birdan olmoqchi bo'lib g'ozni so'yadi va hammasidan ayriladi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik baxt manbasini yo'q qiladi.

рЯУЪ goose /…°uЋРs/ вАФ g'oz; greedy /ЋИ…°riЋРdi/ вАФ ochko'z; fortune /ЋИf…ФЋРt ГuЋРn/ вАФ baxt, boylik

posts.title: The Moon drifts away
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

The Moon is slowly moving away from Earth вАФ about 3.8 cm every year. That is roughly the speed at which your fingernails grow.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Oy Yerdan sekin uzoqlashmoqda вАФ har yili ~3.8 sm. Bu taxminan tirnoq o'sish tezligiga teng.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: move away вАФ uzoqlashmoq; roughly /ЋИr Мfli/ вАФ taxminan; grow /…°r…Щ К/ вАФ o'smoq

posts.title: Paper from China
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Paper was invented in China around 105 AD by Cai Lun. Before paper, people wrote on silk, bamboo, or clay вАФ much heavier and more expensive.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Qog'oz Xitoyda ~105-yilda Tsay Lun tomonidan ixtiro qilingan. Undan oldin odamlar ipak, bambuk yoki loyga yozgan вАФ ancha og'ir va qimmat.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: invent /…™nЋИvent/ вАФ ixtiro qilmoq; expensive /…™kЋИspens…™v/ вАФ qimmat

posts.title: Academic Verbs
posts.text:
рЯФ§ ACADEMIC VERBS ¬Ј C1

рЯУМ demonstrate /ЋИdem…Щnstre…™t/ вАФ namoyish etmoq, ko'rsatmoq
рЯУМ indicate /ЋИ…™nd…™ke…™t/ вАФ bildirmoq, ko'rsatib turmoq
рЯУМ enhance /…™nЋИh…СЋРns/ вАФ kuchaytirmoq, yaxshilamoq

рЯТђ The study demonstrates that reading enhances vocabulary and indicates clear progress.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Tadqiqot o'qish so'z boyligini kuchaytirishini ko'rsatadi va aniq o'sishni bildiradi.

posts.title: Bats can truly fly
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Bats are the only mammals that can truly fly. Other 'flying' animals, like flying squirrels, only glide through the air.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ko'rshapalaklar haqiqatan ucha oladigan yagona sutemizuvchilardir. Boshqa 'uchuvchi' hayvonlar, masalan uchuvchi olmaxonlar, faqat havoda sirpanadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: mammal /ЋИm√¶ml/ вАФ sutemizuvchi; fly /fla…™/ вАФ uchmoq; glide /…°la…™d/ вАФ sirpanmoq

posts.title: The Dog and the Bone
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Dog and Its Reflection

A dog carried a bone across a bridge. In the water he saw his own reflection and thought it was another dog with a bigger bone. He barked to grab it вАФ and dropped his own bone into the river.

рЯТ° Moral: Be content; greed can cost you what you already have.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ It suyakni ko'tarib ko'prikdan o'tar edi. Suvda o'z aksini ko'rib, boshqa it deb o'yladi va tortib olmoqchi bo'lib o'z suyagini suvga tushirib yubordi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik boringni ham yo'qotadi.

рЯУЪ bone /b…Щ Кn/ вАФ suyak; reflection /r…™ЋИflek Гn/ вАФ aks; content /k…ЩnЋИtent/ вАФ qanoatli

posts.title: Bananas share our DNA
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas. All living things use the same basic genetic code, so we have many genes in common with plants.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Insonlar DNK'sining ~60% bananlar bilan umumiy. Barcha tirik mavjudotlar bir xil genetik kodga ega, shuning uchun bizda o'simliklar bilan ham ko'p umumiy genlar bor.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: gene /d ТiЋРn/ вАФ gen; common /ЋИk…Тm…Щn/ вАФ umumiy; living /ЋИl…™v…™≈Л/ вАФ tirik

posts.title: First powered flight
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

The Wright brothers made the first powered flight in 1903. It lasted only 12 seconds and covered about 37 metres вАФ shorter than the wingspan of a modern jet.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Rayt aka-ukalari 1903-yilda birinchi motorli parvozni amalga oshirgan. U atigi 12 soniya davom etib, ~37 metr uchgan вАФ bu zamonaviy samolyot qanotidan ham qisqa.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: flight /fla…™t/ вАФ parvoz; last /l…СЋРst/ вАФ davom etmoq; cover /ЋИk Мv…Щ/ вАФ bosib o'tmoq

posts.title: Idiom: Under the weather
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯ§Т 'under the weather' вАФ o'zini yomon his qilmoq, betobroq

рЯТђ I can't come today; I'm feeling under the weather.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ So'zma-so'z 'ob-havo ostida' emas вАФ ma'nosi 'kasalroq, mazasi yo'q'.

рЯУЪ ill /…™l/ вАФ kasal; tired /ЋИta…™…Щd/ вАФ charchagan

posts.title: Linking Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ LINKING WORDS

рЯУМ however /ha КЋИev…Щ/ вАФ biroq
рЯУМ because /b…™ЋИk…Тz/ вАФ chunki
рЯУМ therefore /ЋИ√∞e…Щf…ФЋР/ вАФ shuning uchun

рЯТђ I studied hard; therefore, I passed. The test was hard, however, I stayed calm.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Bu bog'lovchilar gaplarni mantiqiy bog'laydi va yozma ishni yaxshilaydi.

posts.title: Most of Earth is water
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water, but over 96% of all that water is salty ocean. Only a tiny part is fresh water we can drink.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yer yuzasining ~71% suv bilan qoplangan, lekin bu suvning 96% dan ortig'i sho'r okean. Faqat juda oz qismi ichish mumkin bo'lgan chuchuk suv.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: surface /ЋИs…ЬЋРf…™s/ вАФ yuza; salty /ЋИs…ФЋРlti/ вАФ sho'r; fresh /fre Г/ вАФ chuchuk

posts.title: The Bundle of Sticks
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Bundle of Sticks

An old man gave each son a single stick to break вАФ easy. Then he tied many sticks together and asked them to break the bundle. No one could. 'Together,' he said, 'you cannot be broken.'

рЯТ° Moral: Unity is strength.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Chol o'g'illariga bittadan tayoq beradi вАФ oson sinadi. Keyin bog'lamni berganda, hech kim sindirolmaydi. 'Birlashsangiz, sizni hech kim sindirolmaydi', deydi. Xulosa: birlik вАФ kuch.

рЯУЪ stick /st…™k/ вАФ tayoq; bundle /ЋИb Мndl/ вАФ bog'lam; unity /ЋИjuЋРn…Щti/ вАФ birlik

posts.title: Penguins live in the south
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Wild penguins live almost only in the Southern Hemisphere. You will not find wild penguins at the North Pole вАФ polar bears live there instead.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yovvoyi pingvinlar deyarli faqat Janubiy yarimsharda yashaydi. Shimoliy qutbda yovvoyi pingvin yo'q вАФ u yerda oq ayiqlar yashaydi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: wild /wa…™ld/ вАФ yovvoyi; pole /p…Щ Кl/ вАФ qutb; instead /…™nЋИsted/ вАФ o'rniga

posts.title: First human in space
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space on 12 April 1961. His flight around the Earth lasted just 108 minutes.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yuriy Gagarin 1961-yil 12-aprelda koinotga uchgan birinchi inson bo'ldi. Uning Yer atrofidagi parvozi atigi 108 daqiqa davom etgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: space /spe…™s/ вАФ koinot; flight /fla…™t/ вАФ parvoz; orbit /ЋИ…ФЋРb…™t/ вАФ orbita

posts.title: Strong Adjectives
posts.text:
рЯФ§ STRONG ADJECTIVES

рЯУМ exhausted /…™…°ЋИz…ФЋРst…™d/ вАФ juda charchagan (very tired)
рЯУМ delighted /d…™ЋИla…™t…™d/ вАФ juda xursand (very happy)
рЯУМ terrified /ЋИter…™fa…™d/ вАФ juda qo'rqqan (very scared)

рЯТђ After the exam I was exhausted, but delighted with my score.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Kuchli sifatlar 'very + oddiy sifat' o'rniga ishlatiladi va nutqni boyitadi.

posts.title: Diamonds are carbon
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Diamonds and pencil lead are both made of pure carbon. The only difference is how the atoms are arranged вАФ that is why one is the hardest natural material and the other is soft.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Olmos ham, qalam grafiti ham sof uglerod (carbon). Farqi faqat atomlarning joylashuvida вАФ shuning uchun biri eng qattiq, ikkinchisi yumshoq.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: carbon /ЋИk…СЋРb…Щn/ вАФ uglerod; atom /ЋИ√¶t…Щm/ вАФ atom; arrange /…ЩЋИre…™nd Т/ вАФ joylashtirmoq

posts.title: The Ant and the Dove
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Ant and the Dove

A dove saw an ant drowning in a river and dropped a leaf for it to climb on. Later, a hunter aimed at the dove. The grateful ant bit his foot, the hunter missed, and the dove flew away safely.

рЯТ° Moral: One good turn deserves another.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Kaptar cho'kayotgan chumoliga barg tashlab qutqaradi. Keyin ovchi kaptarga mo'ljal olganda, chumoli uni chaqib, kaptar qutuladi. Xulosa: yaxshilik yaxshilik bilan qaytadi.

рЯУЪ drown /dra Кn/ вАФ cho'kmoq; grateful /ЋИ…°re…™tfl/ вАФ minnatdor; aim /e…™m/ вАФ mo'ljal olmoq

posts.title: Your heart, every day
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Your heart beats around 100,000 times every day, pumping blood through about 100,000 km of blood vessels вАФ enough to circle the Earth more than twice.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Yuragingiz har kuni ~100,000 marta uradi va qonni ~100,000 km qon tomirlari orqali haydaydi вАФ bu Yerni ikki martadan ko'proq o'rab oladi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: beat /biЋРt/ вАФ urmoq; blood vessel /ЋИvesl/ вАФ qon tomir; circle /ЋИs…ЬЋРkl/ вАФ o'rab olmoq

posts.title: Inventions from China
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Four great inventions came from ancient China: paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass. They changed the whole world.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Qadimgi Xitoydan to'rtta buyuk ixtiro chiqqan: qog'oz, bosmaxona, porox va kompas. Ular butun dunyoni o'zgartirgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: invention /…™nЋИven Гn/ вАФ ixtiro; printing /ЋИpr…™nt…™≈Л/ вАФ bosmaxona; compass /ЋИk Мmp…Щs/ вАФ kompas

posts.title: Idiom: Bite the bullet
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯШђ 'bite the bullet' вАФ qiyin ishga jur'at qilib kirishmoq, chidamoq

рЯТђ I was afraid of speaking English, but I bit the bullet and joined the club.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ma'nosi 'noxush, lekin zarur ishni qat'iyat bilan bajarmoq'.

рЯУЪ brave /bre…™v/ вАФ jasur; decision /d…™ЋИs…™ Тn/ вАФ qaror

posts.title: Hottest planet
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Venus is the hottest planet, not Mercury вАФ even though Mercury is closer to the Sun. Venus has a thick atmosphere that traps heat, reaching about 465¬∞C.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Eng issiq sayyora Merkuriy emas, Venera вАФ garchi Merkuriy Quyoshga yaqinroq bo'lsa-da. Venerada issiqlikni ushlab qoluvchi qalin atmosfera bor, harorat ~465¬∞C ga yetadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: planet /ЋИpl√¶n…™t/ вАФ sayyora; atmosphere /ЋИ√¶tm…Щsf…™…Щ/ вАФ atmosfera; trap /tr√¶p/ вАФ ushlab qolmoq

posts.title: Travel Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ VOCABULARY ¬Ј Travel

рЯУМ journey /ЋИd Т…ЬЋРni/ вАФ sayohat, yo'l
рЯУМ luggage /ЋИl М…°…™d Т/ вАФ yuk, chamadon
рЯУМ abroad /…ЩЋИbr…ФЋРd/ вАФ chet elda

рЯТђ My journey abroad was long, but I packed my luggage well.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ 'travel' umumiy so'z, 'journey' bitta yo'l/sayohat, 'trip' qisqa borib-kelish.

posts.title: Town Mouse, Country Mouse
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Town Mouse and the Country Mouse

A town mouse visited his country cousin and laughed at the simple food. He invited him to the city's feasts вАФ but there, cats and dangers never stopped. The country mouse went home, preferring peace to luxury.

рЯТ° Moral: A simple, safe life is better than a rich, fearful one.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Shahar sichqoni qishloqdagi jo'rasini oddiy ovqat uchun mazax qiladi. Lekin shaharda mushuk va xavf to'xtamaydi. Qishloq sichqoni tinchlikni afzal ko'rib uyiga qaytadi.

рЯУЪ feast /fiЋРst/ вАФ ziyofat; danger /ЋИde…™nd Т…Щ/ вАФ xavf; peace /piЋРs/ вАФ tinchlik

posts.title: Snails and sleep
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Some snails can sleep for up to three years when the weather is too dry or cold, slowing their bodies down to survive.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ba'zi shilliqqurtlar ob-havo juda quruq yoki sovuq bo'lganda uch yilgacha uxlay oladi вАФ tanasini sekinlashtirib, omon qoladi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: snail /sne…™l/ вАФ shilliqqurt; survive /s…ЩЋИva…™v/ вАФ omon qolmoq; weather /ЋИwe√∞…Щ/ вАФ ob-havo

posts.title: Comparatives
posts.text:
рЯУЪ GRAMMAR ¬Ј Comparatives

Qisqa sifat + -er: big вЖТ bigger. Uzun sifat: more + sifat: more useful.

рЯТђ English is easier than I thought, but more useful than any other skill.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ 'than' taqqoslashda ishlatiladi. Istisno: good вЖТ better, bad вЖТ worse.

рЯУЪ compare /k…ЩmЋИpe…Щ/ вАФ taqqoslamoq; easy /ЋИiЋРzi/ вАФ oson

posts.title: Registan of Samarkand
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

The Registan in Samarkand was the heart of the medieval city вАФ a public square framed by three grand madrasahs (Islamic schools). Today it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a symbol of Uzbekistan.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Samarqanddagi Registon o'rta asr shaharning markazi bo'lgan вАФ uchta ulug'vor madrasa bilan o'ralgan maydon. Bugun u YuNESKO merosi va O'zbekiston ramzi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: square /skwe…Щ/ вАФ maydon; symbol /ЋИs…™mbl/ вАФ ramz; heritage /ЋИher…™t…™d Т/ вАФ meros

posts.title: Common Collocations
posts.text:
рЯФ§ COLLOCATIONS

рЯУМ make a decision вАФ qaror qabul qilmoq
рЯУМ take a break вАФ tanaffus qilmoq
рЯУМ pay attention вАФ e'tibor bermoq

рЯТђ Pay attention in class, take a short break, then make a decision calmly.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Inglizchada ba'zi fe'l+ot juftliklari doim birga ishlatiladi (collocations) вАФ ularni birga yodlang.

posts.title: Your brain at night
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Your brain is very active while you sleep. During sleep it organises memories and information from the day вАФ which is why good sleep helps you learn and remember.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Miya uxlayotganda ham juda faol. U kun davomidagi xotira va ma'lumotlarni tartibga soladi вАФ shuning uchun yaxshi uyqu o'rganish va eslab qolishga yordam beradi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: active /ЋИ√¶kt…™v/ вАФ faol; organise /ЋИ…ФЋР…°…Щna…™z/ вАФ tartiblamoq; remember /r…™ЋИmemb…Щ/ вАФ eslamoq

posts.title: Idiom: Spill the beans
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯЂШ 'spill the beans' вАФ sirni oshkor qilmoq

рЯТђ Come on, spill the beans вАФ what happened at the meeting?
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ So'zma-so'z 'loviyani to'kmoq' emas вАФ ma'nosi 'sirni aytib qo'ymoq'.

рЯУЪ secret /ЋИsiЋРkr…Щt/ вАФ sir; reveal /r…™ЋИviЋРl/ вАФ oshkor qilmoq

posts.title: Belling the Cat
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј Belling the Cat

The mice wanted to know when the cat was coming, so they decided to tie a bell around its neck. It was a great plan вАФ until one old mouse asked, 'But who will put the bell on the cat?'

рЯТ° Moral: It is easy to suggest a plan; doing it is the hard part.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Sichqonlar mushuk kelishini bilish uchun bo'yniga qo'ng'iroq bog'lashga qaror qiladi. Ammo bir keksa sichqon so'raydi: 'Kim bog'laydi?' Xulosa: reja taklif qilish oson, bajarish qiyin.

рЯУЪ bell /bel/ вАФ qo'ng'iroq; suggest /s…ЩЋИd Тest/ вАФ taklif qilmoq; plan /pl√¶n/ вАФ reja

posts.title: Why the sky is blue
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

The sky is blue because air scatters blue light from the Sun more than other colours. At sunset the light travels farther, so we see red and orange instead.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Osmon ko'k, chunki havo Quyoshdan kelgan ko'k nurni boshqa ranglardan ko'proq sochadi. Kun botishda nur uzoqroq yo'l bosadi, shuning uchun qizil va to'q sariqni ko'ramiz.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: scatter /ЋИsk√¶t…Щ/ вАФ sochmoq; sunset /ЋИs Мnset/ вАФ kun botishi; colour /ЋИk Мl…Щ/ вАФ rang

posts.title: Edison and the light bulb
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

Thomas Edison did not invent the first light bulb, but in 1879 he created one that was practical and long-lasting. He famously said genius is '1% inspiration and 99% perspiration'.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Tomas Edison birinchi lampochkani ixtiro qilmagan, lekin 1879-yilda amaliy va uzoq ishlaydiganini yaratgan. Uning mashhur so'zi: daholik '1% ilhom va 99% mehnat'.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: practical /ЋИpr√¶kt…™kl/ вАФ amaliy; genius /ЋИd ТiЋРni…Щs/ вАФ daho; effort /ЋИef…Щt/ вАФ mehnat

posts.title: Food & Cooking
posts.text:
рЯФ§ VOCABULARY ¬Ј Food

рЯУМ delicious /d…™ЋИl…™ Г…Щs/ вАФ mazali
рЯУМ recipe /ЋИres…Щpi/ вАФ retsept
рЯУМ ingredient /…™nЋИ…°riЋРdi…Щnt/ вАФ masalliq

рЯТђ This recipe needs only five ingredients, and the result is delicious.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ 'recipe' (retsept) va 'receipt' /r…™ЋИsiЋРt/ (chek) вАФ yozilishi o'xshash, ma'nosi boshqa!

posts.title: Elephants and memory
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Elephants have excellent memories. They can remember other elephants and places for many years, and even recognise old friends after a long time apart.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Fillarning xotirasi juda kuchli. Ular boshqa fillar va joylarni yillab eslab qoladi, hatto uzoq ajralishdan keyin ham eski do'stlarini taniydi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: memory /ЋИmem…Щri/ вАФ xotira; remember /r…™ЋИmemb…Щ/ вАФ eslamoq; recognise /ЋИrek…Щ…°na…™z/ вАФ tanimoq

posts.title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing

A wolf put on a sheepskin and slipped in among the flock to hunt easily. But that evening the shepherd, wanting meat, picked the disguised wolf by mistake вАФ and so the trick destroyed the trickster.

рЯТ° Moral: Deception often traps the deceiver.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Bo'ri qo'y terisini kiyib, podaga qo'shiladi. Lekin kechqurun cho'pon go'sht uchun aynan o'sha 'qo'y'ni tanlaydi. Xulosa: hiyla ko'pincha hiylagarning o'zini tutadi.

рЯУЪ disguise /d…™sЋИ…°a…™z/ вАФ niqoblamoq; flock /fl…Тk/ вАФ poda; deceive /d…™ЋИsiЋРv/ вАФ aldamoq

posts.title: Tomato is a fruit
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Botanically, a tomato is a fruit, not a vegetable, because it grows from a flower and contains seeds. In cooking, though, we usually treat it as a vegetable.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Botanika bo'yicha pomidor вАФ meva, sabzavot emas, chunki u guldan o'sadi va urug'i bor. Lekin oshxonada uni sabzavot sifatida ishlatamiz.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: fruit /fruЋРt/ вАФ meva; seed /siЋРd/ вАФ urug'; vegetable /ЋИved Тt…Щbl/ вАФ sabzavot

posts.title: The Silk Road
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY FACT

The Silk Road was a network of trade routes linking China to Europe for over 1,500 years. Cities of Uzbekistan, like Samarkand and Bukhara, were vital stops where goods and ideas were exchanged.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Ipak yo'li 1500 yildan ortiq Xitoyni Yevropa bilan bog'lagan savdo yo'llari tarmog'i edi. Samarqand va Buxoro kabi O'zbekiston shaharlari mol va g'oyalar almashinadigan muhim bekatlar bo'lgan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: trade /tre…™d/ вАФ savdo; route /ruЋРt/ вАФ yo'l; exchange /…™ksЋИt Гe…™nd Т/ вАФ almashmoq

posts.title: Express Certainty
posts.text:
рЯФ§ CERTAINTY ¬Ј C1

рЯУМ undoubtedly / МnЋИda Кt…™dli/ вАФ shubhasiz
рЯУМ presumably /pr…™ЋИzjuЋРm…Щbli/ вАФ ehtimol
рЯУМ arguably /ЋИ…СЋР…°ju…Щbli/ вАФ bahsli ravishda (eng...)

рЯТђ Reading is arguably the best way to learn, and it undoubtedly builds vocabulary.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Bu so'zlar fikrga ishonch yoki ehtiyotkorlik darajasini qo'shadi вАФ IELTS 7.0+ uchun foydali.

posts.title: Hot water tricks
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Ice floats because frozen water is about 9% less dense than liquid water. This is rare вАФ most substances are denser when solid, but water is special, and that is why lakes freeze from the top down.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Muz suzadi, chunki muzlagan suv suyuq suvdan ~9% kam zich. Bu noyob hodisa вАФ ko'p moddalar qattiq holatda zichroq, lekin suv alohida, shuning uchun ko'llar yuzasidan muzlaydi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: dense /dens/ вАФ zich; solid /ЋИs…Тl…™d/ вАФ qattiq; rare /re…Щ/ вАФ noyob

posts.title: Idiom: Cost an arm and a leg
posts.text:
рЯЧ£ IDIOM OF THE DAY

рЯТЄ 'cost an arm and a leg' вАФ juda qimmat turmoq

рЯТђ That new phone costs an arm and a leg!
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ So'zma-so'z emas вАФ ma'nosi 'juda qimmat'. Suhbatda narx haqida ishlatiladi.

рЯУЪ expensive /…™kЋИspens…™v/ вАФ qimmat; price /pra…™s/ вАФ narx

posts.title: The Milkmaid's Dream
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Milkmaid and Her Pail

A girl carried milk on her head and dreamed of selling it, buying eggs, then chickens, then a fine dress. Lost in her daydream, she tossed her head proudly вАФ and spilled all the milk.

рЯТ° Moral: Do not count your chickens before they hatch.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Qiz sut ko'tarib, uni sotib boyish haqida xayol suradi. Xayolga berilib boshini silkitadi va sutni to'kib yuboradi. Xulosa: ish bitmasdan natijani sanama.

рЯУЪ pail /pe…™l/ вАФ chelak; dream /driЋРm/ вАФ orzu; spill /sp…™l/ вАФ to'kmoq

posts.title: Blood is always red
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Human blood is always red, never blue. Veins look blue through the skin only because of how light passes through your body вАФ but the blood inside is dark red.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Inson qoni doim qizil, hech qachon ko'k emas. Tomirlar teri ostida ko'k ko'rinadi, chunki yorug'lik tana orqali shunday o'tadi вАФ lekin ichidagi qon to'q qizil.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: blood /bl Мd/ вАФ qon; vein /ve…™n/ вАФ tomir; skin /sk…™n/ вАФ teri

posts.title: Al-Beruni the scholar
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

Al-Beruni (973-1048), born in Khwarazm, was a brilliant scholar who studied astronomy, geography and history. He estimated the Earth's radius with remarkable accuracy a thousand years ago.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Xorazmda tug'ilgan Abu Rayhon Beruniy (973-1048) astronomiya, geografiya va tarixni o'rgangan buyuk olim. U ming yil avval Yer radiusini hayratlanarli aniqlikda hisoblagan.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: scholar /ЋИsk…Тl…Щ/ вАФ olim; estimate /ЋИest…™me…™t/ вАФ taxminan hisoblamoq; accuracy /ЋИ√¶kj…Щr…Щsi/ вАФ aniqlik

posts.title: will vs going to
posts.text:
рЯУЪ GRAMMAR ¬Ј Future

'will' вАФ shu zahoti qaror yoki bashorat: I will help you.
'going to' вАФ reja yoki ko'rinib turgan natija: I'm going to study tonight.

рЯТђ Look at those clouds вАФ it's going to rain. OK, I'll take an umbrella.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Reja oldindan bor bo'lsa 'going to', shu onda qaror qilsangiz 'will'.

рЯУЪ plan /pl√¶n/ вАФ reja; decision /d…™ЋИs…™ Тn/ вАФ qaror

posts.title: Bees and flowers
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Bees help grow much of our food. As they move from flower to flower for nectar, they carry pollen, which lets many plants and fruits grow.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Asalarilar oziq-ovqatimizning ko'p qismini yetishtirishga yordam beradi. Guldan gulga nektar uchun uchganda, ular changni tashiydi va ko'p o'simlik va mevalar o'sadi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: bee /biЋР/ вАФ asalari; pollen /ЋИp…Тl…Щn/ вАФ chang; grow /…°r…Щ К/ вАФ o'smoq

posts.title: Health Words
posts.text:
рЯФ§ VOCABULARY ¬Ј Health

рЯУМ healthy /ЋИhelќЄi/ вАФ sog'lom
рЯУМ exercise /ЋИeks…Щsa…™z/ вАФ mashq, sport
рЯУМ sleep well вАФ yaxshi uxlamoq

рЯТђ To stay healthy, exercise often, eat well, and sleep well.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ 'healthy' вАФ sog'lom (sifat), 'health' /helќЄ/ вАФ sog'liq (ot).

posts.title: Bibi-Khanym Mosque
posts.text:
рЯПЫ HISTORY ¬Ј Uzbekistan

The Bibi-Khanym Mosque in Samarkand, built in the early 1400s under Amir Temur, was one of the largest mosques of the Islamic world at the time. Its huge entrance still amazes visitors today.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Samarqanddagi Bibixonim masjidi 1400-yillar boshida Amir Temur davrida qurilgan va o'sha davrda islom olamining eng yirik masjidlaridan biri bo'lgan. Uning ulkan darvozasi bugun ham hayratga soladi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: mosque /m…Тsk/ вАФ masjid; entrance /ЋИentr…Щns/ вАФ darvoza, kirish; amaze /…ЩЋИme…™z/ вАФ hayratlantirmoq

posts.title: The Oak and the Reeds
posts.text:
рЯУЦ SHORT STORY ¬Ј The Oak and the Reeds

A proud oak tree mocked the thin reeds that bent in every wind. Then a great storm came. The oak stood stiff and was torn from the ground, while the reeds bent low and survived.

рЯТ° Moral: It is better to bend than to break.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Mag'rur eman shamolda egilgan qamishlarni mazax qiladi. Bo'ron kelganda eman qattiq turib sinadi, qamishlar esa egilib omon qoladi. Xulosa: sinishdan ko'ra egilish yaxshiroq.

рЯУЪ proud /pra Кd/ вАФ mag'rur; bend /bend/ вАФ egilmoq; storm /st…ФЋРm/ вАФ bo'ron

posts.title: More bacteria than cells
posts.text:
рЯФђ SCIENCE FACT

Your body contains trillions of bacteria вАФ roughly as many bacterial cells as human cells. Most of them are helpful, especially in your gut, where they aid digestion.

рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Tanangizda trillionlab bakteriya bor вАФ taxminan inson hujayralari qadar. Ularning ko'pi foydali, ayniqsa ichakda ovqat hazmiga yordam beradi.

рЯУЪ So'zlar: bacteria /b√¶kЋИt…™…Щri…Щ/ вАФ bakteriya; cell /sel/ вАФ hujayra; digestion /da…™ЋИd Тest Г…Щn/ вАФ hazm

posts.title: Word Form Quiz
posts.text:
рЯУЭ VOCABULARY TEST

Choose the correct word:

Her English improved ____ after daily practice.

A) significant
B) significantly
C) significance
D) signify

рЯТ≠ Javob рЯСЗ
posts.answer:
вЬЕ To'g'ri javob: B) significantly

рЯУЦ Fe'lni (improved) izohlash uchun ravish (adverb) kerak: significantly. A вАФ sifat, C вАФ ot, D вАФ fe'l.

posts.title: Never Give Up
posts.text:
рЯФ§ MOTIVATION WORDS

рЯУМ persist /p…ЩЋИs…™st/ вАФ qat'iyat bilan davom etmoq
рЯУМ progress /ЋИpr…Щ К…°res/ вАФ taraqqiyot
рЯУМ succeed /s…ЩkЋИsiЋРd/ вАФ muvaffaqiyatga erishmoq

рЯТђ If you persist a little every day, you will see progress and finally succeed.
рЯЗЇрЯЗњ Har kuni ozgina qat'iyat bilan ishlasangiz, taraqqiyotni ko'rasiz va muvaffaqiyatga erishasiz.
